acceptanceCriteria: docs/test-integrity.md catalogs assertion deletion/relaxation, tolerance widening, skip/only/todo, case removal, snapshot churn, mock substitution, coverage decline, mutation-score decline, and configured-check weakening as bounded threats.

Every cataloged signal is classified by confidence, language/framework dependence, false-positive risk, required context, and possible enforcement level, and is explicitly labeled a deterministic fact, a tool-derived measurement, or an advisory interpretation.
Every proposed signal either has a falsifiable test (a concrete repository change that must fire it and a concrete benign change that must not) or is explicitly rejected with the reason.
docs/test-integrity.md defines attacker and accidental-change scenarios relevant to Rigor attempts.

docs/test-integrity.md specifies a minimal shadow-mode event format that records signals without enforcing them, aligned with existing artifact conventions (no raw command output, repository-relative paths, opaque digests).
docs/test-integrity.md explains how the catalog interacts with the #13 failure-fingerprint/progress comparison (weakeningSignals, fail-closed reduced) and with #16 outcome-based calibration.
High-confidence candidates for shadow-mode collection (#22) are explicitly identified and distinguished from rejected or advisory-only signals.

docs/test-integrity.md states what must never be advertised as a semantic guarantee, and docs/threat-model.md and docs/mvp-limitations.md reference the catalog while remaining honest that semantic test quality is not guaranteed.
CHANGELOG.md documents the design under an Unreleased heading following the one-entry-per-issue convention.
npm run test:all, npm run bench, and claude plugin validate . --strict pass, and git diff --check is clean.
note: Policy permits transmission; minimize content and re-check destination controls.
